Oh, to come so close, yet be held back for want of runes!

When you reach level 5 in a role you unlock the Supreme version of these titles for whichever role you have mastered.

If you have mastered all four roles, and killed both the king and queen, then you are eligible for the Penance Slayer title.

All of these extra titles are available from the customisation menu too.

684.name: Watch out for rabbits wandering around the farmyard. They are hungry and will look for flags, as they often mean hidden food! If a rabbit eats the seed under a flag, you will not get any points for it and will lose the flag.

687.name: Use a spade to dig in the farmer's field and try to work out where the seeds have been planted. Try not to dig up the seeds, as this will lose you points! Usually, when you dig a square, you will uncover a number, which shows how many seeds are in the holes next to it.

688.name: While you are in the area, you will be able to see your points total in the top-right corner of your screen. Your points are saved, so you can leave if you wish and return another time to pick up from where you left off.

690.name: Farmers wander the area looking for seeds that have been dug up by mistake. They will also collect any flags you have placed, resetting the nearby area and giving you points if the flag is in the right place.

719.name:
COAL AND WATER SUPPLIES:
For your convenience, the incubator has a coal scuttle and water tank, which can hold up to twelve buckets of coal or water respectively. Simply use a bucket of coal or water on the coal scuttle or water tank to add to the supplies.

ADJUSTING THE INCUBATOR TEMPERATURE:
The control panel to the left of the incubator allows you to use the coal and water supplies you have added to the incubator to heat or cool it.

INCUBATING AN EGG:
> Place a suitable egg inside the incubation chamber.
> Add supplies to the water tank and coal scuttle
> Using the incubator controls, adjust the temperature. The temperature gauge has a needle which shows the current temperature. The top of the temperature gauge also has an arrow showing the temperature needed to reach the next phase of incubation.
> Some eggs may require you to reach several different temperatures before they hatch.

The very best of luck with hatching your eggs!

928.name: Once Upon a Time in Gielinor: Finale

930.name:
Welcome to your factory!

You have to fulfil an order of 7 Fruitcakes, 7 Nutty Eggs and 7 Tangy Egg Breads for the Easter Bunny. If you can complete the order in less that 15 turns, you will receive an additional reward for your hard work.

You start with 5 workers.

You can purchase more workers for 2 random resources each.

You can have a maximum of 15 workers employed at any time.

Every turn you pay 1 resource as wages for every 5 workers you have.

Workers can be allocated to gathering resources, making orders or repairing machines.

You can have up to 15 of each resource stored. Resources generated over 15 are lost.

Each finished product takes 2 resources to make.

Fruit Cake needs: 1 Chocolate, 1 Fruit.

Nutty Egg needs: 1 Chocolate, 1 Nuts.

Tangy Egg Bread needs 1 Fruit, 1 Nuts.

Every turn, there is a chance that something might befall the factory. You may lose some resources, find some bonus resources, or one of your machines might be in need of repair. This will be reported to you, as well as all the other details you need to know in running your factory, when you end your turn.

If a machine is broken, you can choose to alllocate a worker to repair it, or you can repair it yourself by going to the machine and performing the repair, as you have done so already. Machines that are not repaired will produce 1 less item per turn.

When you have completed the order you will be shown your score. The lower the Score, the better you have performed. Your score is broken down into the various parts that make it up. Why not see if you can beat your friends score.

You can repeat the game as many times as you like during the event.



Best of luck!

The complexity of a dungeon determines how many of your skills and abilities will be tested in a raid. Choosing a lower complexity will make the dungeon more straight-forward to complete, but it will also lower the Dungeoneering XP reward you receive at the end.

When you first start Dungeoneering, only complexity 1 will be unlocked. You must complete at least one raid at that complexity before you can unlock the next.

The ruins stand in honour of Het and in memory of the many combatants who fought here.

The numbers around the dance floor relate to the row or column they are lined up with, and tell you how many adjacent tiles within that row or column need to be turned on.

For instance, a row with 10 next to it would need all ten tiles in that row turned on; or a column with 4 next to it means four tiles need turning on in that column.

1667.name: It’s time to take your adventuring exam, and you are super nervous. The paper seems to be covered in random scribbles and unreadable symbols, which is making it pretty tricky to answer the questions. Worse, you keep on having to stop the exam in order to go to adventuring lessons.  This is totally stupid and a waste of time as you’ve also got to get ready to go adventuring.  You scamper up and down the halls of adventuring school, checking your schedule and getting more and more frustrated as you discover dozens of subjects you’ve not attended for months are now due for final exams.  But wait…none of this makes any sense!  You’ve been an adventurer for years, and even then you didn’t go to a school to learn how to do it.  This must be a crazy dream.  That explains why all the teachers are clouds of shrieking bats.  You wake up with a start.

1704.name:

1724.name:
From far below came strains of gruff, tuneless humming. I rubbed my eyes, and peered down through the branches to see a stocky dwarf staggering towards the tree's roots. He leant against the trunk with one hand, chuckled to himself – as if at some private joke – and fumbled with his breeches. There was a trickling sound, and I grimaced.

Ugh…right. After this, I can sleep?

Silence. Good enough for me.

I clambered up into the high branches, and squinted around until I found a giant leaf, still welling with water from the night's rain. I flipped it with the end of my staff, and rainwater gushed down to the sound of dwarven curses.

I lay back in the morning sun and laughed, the boughs bobbing in time.

1818.name:
Sliske has placed you and the other gods into a Labyrinth that seems to span as far as your eyes can see.

There are two stages of the Labyrinth and each stage has been split into four sections.

The exit of the Labyrinth is always in the most north-easterly room of the north east section.

Some parts of the Labyrinth seem to be impassable because that room needs to be rotated. If you encounter one of these rooms you will need to find and complete one of Sliske's devious puzzle rooms to rotate it to the correct position.

Beware: You are racing against all the other gods and the longer you take working out your path, the further ahead they get. Time stands still when you are in a room, but advances when you leave through an archway. Choose your steps carefully.
